original_hypothesis.title: SignalService.sendSignal allows anyone to write arbitrary signal slots, enabling state bloat and potentially increased proof costs (storage-grief)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: SignalService.sendSignal is permissionless and stores _signal as value in a deterministic slot derived from (chainid, msg.sender, _signal). An attacker can spam sendSignal with many unique signals, permanently increasing contract storage and potentially increasing state sync overhead or archive-node costs. While not directly breaking proofs (slots are namespaced by app=msg.sender), it can be an on-chain storage grief vector against the system-wide SignalService singleton.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a practical chain-level grief risk; SignalService is unpausable by design and is a shared dependency. Even if storage writes are paid by attacker, the long-term operational burden is borne by node operators and could affect protocol reliability.

proof_of_signal.pattern: permissionless storage writes with attacker-controlled keyspace (unique _signal values)

preconditions: Attacker is willing to pay gas for many storage writes
SignalService is a shared singleton used by many apps/bridges

initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no rate limit, no fee mechanism beyond gas, no pruning

analysis_questions: Is SignalService intended to be spam-resistant at L2 gas costs?
Are there any protocol operations whose gas depends on SignalService state size (should not, but verify)?
validation_requirements: Confirm no loops over stored signals exist anywhere
Estimate cost/feasibility of meaningful bloat on target chain gas pricing

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker submits many sendSignal transactions generating unique slots, bloating SignalService storage. Over time, this increases state size and may degrade node performance.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: No direct fund theft; potential ecosystem-wide operational cost externality
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All protocol users indirectly (node performance, indexing)
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Difficult (state is permanent); mitigation via protocol upgrade and/or L2 spam controls

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No stress tests around SignalService storage growth

technical_investigation_focus.gas_analysis_needed: estimate gas per sstore and feasible spam rate under L2 conditions
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: monitor SignalSent event rate and unique slot growth

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
